questions.question: Which state has become the second in India to publish a comprehensive state-level Bird Atlas?
questions.explanation: The Bird Atlas of Goa was released at the ninth Bird Festival of Goa, making Goa the second state after Kerala to publish a state-level bird atlas. The festival also saw the release of “Birds of Goa: Konkani Nomenclature – Olakh Suknayanchi,” promoting bird knowledge in the local language. The ninth Bird Festival of Goa, themed “Majestic Mhadei,” was inaugurated at Valpoi. Volunteers, bird conservationists, forest staff, and wildlife rescuers were honoured for their contributions to avian conservation.

questions.question: Which country won the South Asian Football Federation (SAFF) Under-19 Women’s Championship title 2026?
questions.explanation: India defeated Bangladesh 4–0 to win the South Asian Football Federation (SAFF) Under-19 Women’s Championship. The final was played at Pokhara Rangasala Stadium in Nepal. Captain Julan Nongmaithem scored the opening goal for India. Elizabeth Lakra, Pearl Fernandes, and substitute Anwita Raghuraman added the remaining goals. India fielded its Under-17 women’s national team to prepare for the Asian Football Confederation (AFC) Under-17 Women’s Asian Cup later this year.

questions.question: Thwaites Glacier, that was recently seen in news, is located in which region?
questions.explanation: Scientists warned that the Thwaites Glacier is melting rapidly due to human-induced climate change. It is a massive glacier in West Antarctica, often called the Doomsday Glacier. The glacier is very wide and fast-flowing, roughly the size of Florida or Great Britain. It is extremely important because it already contributes about 4% to global annual sea-level rise. Studies show it is thinning, retreating, and adding more meltwater to the oceans. If it collapses, it could destabilise the West Antarctic Ice Sheet, causing severe sea-level rise.

questions.question: What is the rank of India in the Network Readiness Index 2025 (NRI 2025)?
questions.explanation: India improved its rank by four places to 45th in the Network Readiness Index (NRI) 2025, released on 4 February 2026. The index assesses 127 economies across four pillars: Technology, People, Governance, and Impact, using 53 indicators. The report is prepared by the Portulans Institute, a non-profit research body based in Washington DC. India’s score increased from 53.63 in 2024 to 54.43 in 2025 out of 100. India ranked 1st in telecom investment, Artificial Intelligence (AI) publications, Information and Communication Technology (ICT) exports, and e-commerce laws. It ranked 2nd in Fibre to the Home (FTTH) subscriptions and internet traffic, showing strong digital growth.

questions.question: HbA1c test, that was recently seen in news, is commonly used in India for diagnosing which disease?
questions.explanation: A new study shows that glycated hemoglobin (HbA1c), widely used in India to detect type-2 diabetes, may not always show the true blood glucose level. Hemoglobin A1c (HbA1c) is a blood test that tells the average blood sugar level of the last two to three months. The test measures the percentage of red blood cells (RBCs) whose hemoglobin is coated with glucose. This works because glucose sticks to hemoglobin for the full life of RBCs, which is about three months. A high HbA1c value means long-term high blood sugar, indicating diabetes. Diabetes can lead to heart, kidney, and nerve diseases.

questions.question: Which country launched iLive Connect, the world’s first doctor-led Artificial Intelligence (AI) healthcare ecosystem?
questions.explanation: India launched iLive Connect, the world’s first doctor-led Artificial Intelligence (AI) healthcare ecosystem for continuous home-based clinical monitoring. It bridges gaps in chronic disease care and post-hospital recovery, especially for elderly and remote-area patients. The system uses medical-grade wearable biosensors, a chest patch and wristband, to track ECG, heart rate, respiration, oxygen saturation (SpO₂), temperature, blood pressure trends, activity, sleep, and heart rate variability. Data is sent in real time to a 24×7 medical command centre staffed by specialist doctors.

questions.question: The Buddy Squadron aerial exercise is conducted between South Korea and which country?
questions.explanation: South Korean and United States air forces have started the Buddy Squadron joint aerial exercise at Osan Air Base in Pyeongtaek. The exercise aims to improve interoperability and integrated tactical skills between the two allies. Buddy Squadron is a battalion-level drill conducted alternately by South Korea and the United States. It focuses on sharing air-to-air combat tactics and integrated training. The exercise involves fourth- and fifth-generation fighter jets. It strengthens joint operational readiness and regional security cooperation.

questions.question: As per Ministry of New and Renewable Energy, what is India’s position globally in terms of renewable energy production?
questions.explanation: India has achieved a total renewable energy capacity of 272 gigawatts, as stated by the Minister for New and Renewable Energy in the Rajya Sabha. Solar energy capacity has grown sharply from 2.8 gigawatts to 136 gigawatts. India has now become the world’s third-largest renewable energy producer. The Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan (PM-KUSUM) scheme is benefiting rural areas through solar pumps and solarised agriculture. Several states are seeking expansion of the scheme due to its success. India is leading globally in green hydrogen, with 21 projects producing 8,300 tonnes per year commissioned so far.

questions.question: What is the rank of India in the 2025 Corruption Perceptions Index (CPI)?
questions.explanation: Global corruption is rising as per Transparency International’s 2025 Corruption Perceptions Index (CPI). Corruption Perceptions Index (CPI) measures perceived public sector corruption in 182 countries. It uses a scale from 0 (highly corrupt) to 100 (very clean). Only 5 countries scored above 80, down from 12 a decade ago. The global average score fell to 42, the lowest in over ten years. 122 countries scored below 50, showing serious corruption concerns. Denmark ranked first with 89; South Sudan and Somalia ranked last with 9. India ranked 91st with a score of 39, showing slight improvement.

questions.question: Ayushman Sahakar Scheme is implemented by which organization?

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

questions.explanation: National Cooperative Development Corporation (NCDC), a statutory body under Ministry of Cooperation, implements Ayushman Sahakar Scheme to promote cooperative healthcare. The scheme aligns with National Health Policy 2017 to provide affordable and holistic healthcare through cooperatives. Financial assistance is given as term loan or investment loan after technical and financial appraisal. The scheme supports hospitals, AYUSH, medical colleges, telemedicine, digital health and insurance services. Loan period is up to 8 years with moratorium, and women-majority cooperatives get 1% interest rebate. The initiative promotes community participation and sustainable cooperative healthcare delivery.
